problems.name: Motor Failure
problems.description: One or more motors stop spinning during flight or fail to spin on startup, causing unstable flight or crash.
visual_symptoms: Drone tilts aggressively to one side during flight
One motor visibly not spinning at startup
Burnt smell from motor housing
Rapid altitude loss or uncontrolled rotation

probable_causes.cause: Motor windings burnt due to overcurrent or crash damage
probable_causes.test: Spin each motor by hand — excessive resistance or grinding indicates burnt windings

probable_causes.cause: Motor bearings seized from dirt, water ingress or wear
probable_causes.test: Spin motor shaft by hand to check for roughness or wobble

probable_causes.cause: ESC signal wire disconnected or damaged
probable_causes.test: Inspect ESC-to-motor connector for loose pins or broken solder joints

steps.instruction: Power off the drone and remove all propellers before inspection
steps.verification: All props removed and power physically disconnected

steps.instruction: Spin each motor shaft by hand to identify the faulty motor — look for resistance, grinding or wobble
steps.verification: Faulty motor identified by feel or visual check

steps.instruction: Unsolder the three motor wires from the ESC and replace the faulty motor with an identical replacement
steps.verification: New motor spins freely by hand with no wobble

steps.instruction: Solder new motor wires to ESC, power on and run motor test in flight controller software
steps.verification: Motor spins at correct RPM in correct direction without vibration

problems.name: ESC Burning / Failure
problems.description: Electronic Speed Controller overheats, emits smoke, or fails to drive a motor, causing loss of control.
visual_symptoms: Smoke or burning smell from ESC housing
One motor not responding to throttle inputs
Discoloured or melted ESC casing
Drone spins uncontrollably on takeoff

probable_causes.cause: Overcurrent event from motor blockage or crash impact
probable_causes.test: Check ESC current rating vs motor peak draw; inspect for physical damage on ESC board

probable_causes.cause: Inadequate cooling causing thermal runaway during sustained high-throttle flight
probable_causes.test: Check if ESC has adequate airflow; measure post-flight ESC temperature with IR thermometer

probable_causes.cause: Voltage spike from battery during rapid throttle changes
probable_causes.test: Check capacitor condition on ESC and battery C-rating versus peak draw

steps.instruction: Remove battery and inspect ESC visually for burnt components, discolouration or melted plastic
steps.verification: Damage localised to specific ESC confirmed

steps.instruction: Desolder and remove the burnt ESC; replace with ESC of identical or higher current rating
steps.verification: New ESC installed with clean solder joints on all pads

steps.instruction: Flash ESC firmware using BLHeli Suite or ESC Configurator to match flight controller settings
steps.verification: Firmware version confirmed in configurator; motor direction correct

steps.instruction: Perform throttle calibration in flight controller and run motor test at low throttle
steps.verification: Motor spins smoothly; no heat buildup at idle throttle after 60 seconds

problems.name: LiPo Battery Swelling
problems.description: The LiPo battery pack shows visible inflation or puffing, indicating internal cell damage and risk of fire.
visual_symptoms: Battery case visibly bulged or puffed outward
Battery no longer fits snugly in battery bay
Reduced flight time compared to normal
Battery feels warm even when not in use

probable_causes.cause: Overcharging beyond 4.2V per cell causing gas buildup
probable_causes.test: Measure cell voltages with multimeter — any cell above 4.25V indicates overcharge damage

probable_causes.cause: Over-discharging below 3.0V per cell damaging cell chemistry
probable_causes.test: Check if battery was ever discharged to low-battery cutoff repeatedly; measure resting cell voltages

probable_causes.cause: Physical impact damage to cells from crash
probable_causes.test: Inspect battery exterior for dents or deformation caused by impact

steps.instruction: Immediately stop using the swollen battery and remove it from the drone
steps.verification: Battery physically separated from drone and stored in a fireproof LiPo bag

steps.instruction: Place battery in a LiPo-safe bag outdoors and allow it to discharge fully using a LiPo discharger set to storage discharge
steps.verification: All cells read 3.0V or below before disposal

steps.instruction: Dispose of the discharged battery at a certified e-waste or battery recycling facility
steps.verification: Battery deposited at recycling point; receipt or confirmation obtained

problems.name: GPS Signal Loss
problems.description: The drone cannot acquire or maintain sufficient GPS satellite lock, disabling position hold, return-to-home and autonomous flight modes.
visual_symptoms: Flight controller app shows fewer than 6 satellites
GPS status LED flashing rather than solid
Drone drifts laterally in altitude-hold mode
RTH and waypoint modes unavailable in app

probable_causes.cause: GPS module obstructed by carbon fibre frame or metal components
probable_causes.test: Move GPS module to highest point on drone with clear sky view and retest satellite count

probable_causes.cause: GPS antenna cable damaged or disconnected
probable_causes.test: Inspect U.FL or SMA connector on GPS module; check cable continuity with multimeter

probable_causes.cause: Electromagnetic interference from power wires or video transmitter
probable_causes.test: Increase distance between GPS module and power cables; check satellite count with VTX off

steps.instruction: Move the GPS module on a mast to at least 5 cm above the top plate, away from carbon fibre
steps.verification: GPS module has unobstructed 360-degree sky view; mast secure and vibration-free

steps.instruction: Inspect and reseat GPS antenna cable connector; replace cable if damaged
steps.verification: Cable connector clicks securely; no kinks or exposed wiring

steps.instruction: Power on drone in open sky and wait for satellite acquisition; confirm 8+ satellites in flight controller
steps.verification: Satellite count stable at 8 or above within 90 seconds

problems.name: Camera Gimbal Malfunction
problems.description: The camera gimbal vibrates, drifts to one side, or fails to stabilise video footage during flight.
visual_symptoms: Camera tilted and not self-levelling at startup
Jello or rolling-shutter artefacts in recorded video
Gimbal making clicking or grinding noises
Gimbal motor running hot after short use

probable_causes.cause: Gimbal IMU needs recalibration after firmware update or impact
probable_causes.test: Run gimbal calibration routine in drone app; check if horizon drifts after calibration

probable_causes.cause: Gimbal motor bearing damaged from crash or hard landing
probable_causes.test: Manually move camera axis through full range and feel for binding or roughness

probable_causes.cause: Gimbal ribbon cable damaged causing signal interruption
probable_causes.test: Inspect ribbon cable for kinks, cuts or loose ZIF connector seating

steps.instruction: Open drone app, navigate to gimbal settings and run auto-calibration on a flat level surface
steps.verification: Calibration completes without error; horizon level confirmed in live view

steps.instruction: If calibration fails, inspect ribbon cable and reseat ZIF connector on both ends
steps.verification: Ribbon cable seated flat with no visible kinks; lock lever fully engaged

steps.instruction: Test gimbal stability by hovering at eye level and panning the drone 90 degrees
steps.verification: Camera horizon remains stable within 1 degree during pan

problems.name: Propeller Vibration
problems.description: Excessive vibration from unbalanced or damaged propellers causes jello in video and stress on frame components.
visual_symptoms: Jello distortion visible in recorded footage
Drone shakes visibly at hover
Unusual buzzing sound at specific throttle levels
Frame screws loosening frequently

probable_causes.cause: Propeller blade chipped, cracked or bent from debris strike
probable_causes.test: Inspect each prop under bright light for chips, cracks or asymmetric blade shape

probable_causes.cause: Propeller mass imbalance from manufacturing variation
probable_causes.test: Use a propeller balancer to measure static imbalance on each prop

probable_causes.cause: Motor shaft bent causing eccentric rotation
probable_causes.test: Mount prop-less motor and run at low speed; observe shaft tip for wobble

steps.instruction: Remove all propellers and inspect each blade for chips, cracks or asymmetry; replace any damaged props
steps.verification: All four props visually intact with uniform blade shape

steps.instruction: Balance replacement props using a magnetic propeller balancer; add tape to lighter blade until balanced
steps.verification: Propeller rests horizontal on balancer without rotating in any position

steps.instruction: Reinstall balanced props and conduct a hover test; review footage for jello reduction
steps.verification: No visible jello in 1080p test footage at hover

problems.name: Compass Calibration Failure
problems.description: The drone fails compass calibration or shows persistent heading errors, causing drift and incorrect RTH direction.
visual_symptoms: Flight controller shows compass error warning on startup
Drone heading indicator in app does not match real-world direction
Drone drifts sideways in GPS mode despite no wind
Toilet-bowling circular drift pattern during hover

probable_causes.cause: Magnetic interference from nearby metal objects or power cables during calibration
probable_causes.test: Repeat calibration outdoors away from metal structures, cars and power lines

probable_causes.cause: Compass module hardware failure or physical damage
probable_causes.test: Check compass raw values in flight controller software — should show smooth sine wave as drone is rotated

probable_causes.cause: Compass module mounted too close to ESCs or battery causing permanent interference
probable_causes.test: Compare compass heading with GPS heading at identical orientation — consistent offset indicates mounting issue

steps.instruction: Move to an open area at least 20 m from metal structures, vehicles and power lines
steps.verification: No large metal objects within 20 m confirmed visually

steps.instruction: Follow flight controller app compass calibration routine: rotate drone on all three axes as instructed
steps.verification: Calibration completes with success notification and no error codes

steps.instruction: Verify calibration by slowly rotating the powered drone and confirming heading in app tracks real-world direction within 5 degrees
steps.verification: Heading error below 5 degrees across full 360-degree rotation

problems.name: RC Link Dropout
problems.description: The radio control link between transmitter and drone cuts out intermittently or completely, triggering failsafe.
visual_symptoms: RC signal indicator drops to zero in flight controller telemetry
Drone activates failsafe RTH without pilot input
Stick inputs lag or have no effect momentarily
Transmitter shows signal loss alert

probable_causes.cause: Receiver antenna positioned incorrectly or damaged
probable_causes.test: Inspect receiver antennas for damage; ensure two antennas are oriented 90 degrees apart

probable_causes.cause: 2.4 GHz or 5.8 GHz RF interference from nearby WiFi or other drones
probable_causes.test: Use spectrum analyser app to identify congested channels; switch to less-used frequency channel

probable_causes.cause: Receiver or transmitter firmware mismatch causing protocol errors
probable_causes.test: Check receiver and transmitter firmware versions match; rebind using current firmware

steps.instruction: Inspect receiver antennas and reposition so two antennas form a 90-degree V-shape; replace if damaged
steps.verification: Antennas physically intact and correctly angled; signal bars consistent

steps.instruction: Update transmitter and receiver firmware to matching latest versions and rebind
steps.verification: Bind confirmed in flight controller; link quality shows above 95% at 50 m

steps.instruction: Perform a range test at 50 m and 100 m, verifying no dropouts
steps.verification: Zero signal loss events recorded in telemetry log during range test

problems.name: FPV Video Glitch
problems.description: The FPV live video feed shows static, rolling lines, loss of image or colour distortion.
visual_symptoms: Static lines or white noise appearing in FPV feed
Video feed cuts out at certain flight orientations
Colour bands or hue shifts in image
Video freezes while drone is still flying

probable_causes.cause: VTX power supply noise from ESC switching interference
probable_causes.test: Add LC filter on VTX power input; observe if glitch correlates with throttle changes

probable_causes.cause: Camera or VTX antenna cable intermittent connection
probable_causes.test: Wiggle camera connector and SMA antenna connector while powered; observe if glitch appears

probable_causes.cause: VTX overheating causing output power reduction
probable_causes.test: Measure VTX temperature after 5-minute flight with IR thermometer; above 70°C indicates overheating

steps.instruction: Install an LC filter (1000uF capacitor + ferrite bead) on the VTX power supply wire
steps.verification: Clean DC supply measured on VTX power rail; glitch frequency reduced on bench test

steps.instruction: Inspect and reseat all camera and VTX antenna connectors; replace any with bent pins
steps.verification: All connectors fully seated; wiggle test produces no video interruption

steps.instruction: Ensure VTX has adequate airflow or reduce output power to stay below 200 mW in close-range flying
steps.verification: VTX temperature below 65°C after 10-minute flight; clear video throughout

problems.name: Return-to-Home Failure
problems.description: The automatic return-to-home function fails to activate, navigates to the wrong location, or overshoots the home point.
visual_symptoms: Drone does not respond to RTH command from transmitter
RTH activates but drone flies in wrong direction
Home point indicator in app shows incorrect location
Drone overshoots home point and hovers offset

probable_causes.cause: Home point not recorded at takeoff due to insufficient GPS lock
probable_causes.test: Check flight log for GPS satellite count at moment of takeoff — less than 8 satellites is insufficient

probable_causes.cause: Compass error causing incorrect heading reference for RTH navigation
probable_causes.test: Verify compass calibration status; check for persistent compass error flags in flight controller

probable_causes.cause: RTH altitude set below obstacle height causing collision avoidance intervention
probable_causes.test: Confirm RTH altitude setting in app is at least 10 m above tallest nearby obstacle

steps.instruction: Before every flight, wait for satellite count to reach 8 or more before arming motors
steps.verification: Flight controller app shows 8+ satellites and home point set notification

steps.instruction: Set RTH altitude in drone app to at least 15 m above the highest nearby obstacle
steps.verification: RTH altitude value saved in app and confirmed in safety settings

steps.instruction: Perform a test RTH at low altitude (5 m) in open area to verify home point accuracy
steps.verification: Drone returns within 2 m of takeoff point

problems.name: Crash Detection False Trigger
problems.description: The drone incorrectly detects a crash during normal flight, cutting motor power and causing an actual crash.
visual_symptoms: Motors suddenly cut out without pilot input during flight
Flight controller log shows crash-detected event at normal flight parameters
Drone drops unexpectedly during aggressive manoeuvres
Error code for crash detection in post-flight log

probable_causes.cause: Crash detection threshold set too sensitive for acro or windy flight
probable_causes.test: Review flight controller crash detection sensitivity setting; lower threshold and retest

probable_causes.cause: IMU vibration causing false G-force spike triggering crash detection
probable_causes.test: Review IMU vibration logs; spikes above 3G during normal flight indicate soft-mount or prop balance issue

probable_causes.cause: Firmware bug in crash detection algorithm
probable_causes.test: Check flight controller community forums for known crash detection bugs in current firmware version

steps.instruction: Open flight controller configurator and increase crash detection threshold or disable it for freestyle/acro flight
steps.verification: Setting saved; test hover without false trigger

steps.instruction: Soft-mount the flight controller using rubber dampers to reduce vibration transmission from motors
steps.verification: IMU vibration log shows peaks below 2G during normal hover

steps.instruction: Update flight controller firmware to latest stable release to benefit from crash detection algorithm fixes
steps.verification: Firmware update successful; no crash detection events during 5-minute test flight

problems.name: Charging Port Damage
problems.description: The battery or drone charging port is physically damaged, preventing proper connection to charger.
visual_symptoms: Charger plug does not seat firmly in port
Charging indicator does not activate when charger is connected
Bent or missing pins visible in port connector
Intermittent charging — starts and stops repeatedly

probable_causes.cause: Physical damage from inserting charger at wrong angle or with excessive force
probable_causes.test: Inspect port under magnification for bent pins, cracks or foreign debris

probable_causes.cause: Corrosion on port contacts from moisture or sweat exposure
probable_causes.test: Use cotton swab with isopropyl alcohol to clean port; check if charging resumes

probable_causes.cause: Port solder joints cracked on PCB from mechanical stress
probable_causes.test: Apply slight sideways pressure to port while charging — if connection intermittently restores, solder joints are cracked

steps.instruction: Use a wooden toothpick to carefully straighten any bent pins in the port, and clean with isopropyl alcohol on a cotton swab
steps.verification: Pins straight and clean; charger seats with normal resistance

steps.instruction: If pins are broken or port is cracked, desolder and replace the charging port connector with matching replacement
steps.verification: New port firmly attached; charger connects securely and charging indicator activates

problems.name: Overheating During Flight
problems.description: Motors, ESCs or the flight controller reach dangerously high temperatures during flight, risking component failure.
visual_symptoms: Components too hot to touch immediately after landing
Thermal protection warning in flight controller app
Reduced performance and lower top speed during flight
Burning smell after landing

probable_causes.cause: Prolonged high-throttle flight exceeding motor or ESC thermal rating
probable_causes.test: Measure component temperatures with IR thermometer after flight; above 80°C for motors or ESCs is critical

probable_causes.cause: Partially blocked cooling airflow due to cable routing obstruction
probable_causes.test: Inspect frame for cables obstructing airflow over ESCs and motors

probable_causes.cause: Motor KV rating mismatched to propeller size causing excessive current draw
probable_causes.test: Measure no-load current draw per motor at full throttle; exceeding motor spec by more than 20% indicates mismatch

steps.instruction: Route all cables away from motor and ESC surfaces to restore cooling airflow; use cable ties to secure
steps.verification: Unobstructed airflow path visible over all ESCs and motor bodies

steps.instruction: Reduce flight duration and add a mandatory 5-minute cool-down between flights when temperatures exceed 70°C
steps.verification: Motor temperature below 60°C at start of each subsequent flight

steps.instruction: Verify motor KV and propeller pitch pairing using prop calculator; switch to lower-pitch props if current is excessive
steps.verification: Full-throttle current per motor within manufacturer specification

problems.name: Firmware Brick
problems.description: The drone or flight controller becomes unresponsive after a failed or incompatible firmware update.
visual_symptoms: Flight controller does not appear as USB device when connected to PC
All LEDs off or stuck in single colour pattern at boot
Configurator software cannot connect to flight controller
Drone unresponsive to transmitter bind attempts

probable_causes.cause: USB disconnected or power lost during firmware write operation
probable_causes.test: Check flight controller bootloader mode by holding boot button during USB connect; if bootloader appears, flash recovery is possible

probable_causes.cause: Incompatible firmware version or wrong target flashed
probable_causes.test: Identify exact flight controller hardware revision; verify firmware target file matches hardware markings

probable_causes.cause: Corrupted download of firmware file
probable_causes.test: Verify MD5/SHA256 checksum of firmware file against official repository

steps.instruction: Hold the boot/DFU button on the flight controller while connecting USB to enter bootloader mode
steps.verification: Device appears in PC device manager as DFU or STM32 bootloader device

steps.instruction: Download the correct firmware binary for the exact flight controller target and verify its checksum
steps.verification: Checksum matches official release; correct hardware target confirmed

steps.instruction: Flash firmware using Betaflight Configurator full-erase flash or STM32CubeProgrammer in DFU mode
steps.verification: Flash completes at 100% without error; device reboots and appears as flight controller in configurator

steps.instruction: Reconfigure all settings from scratch or restore from saved configuration backup
steps.verification: Motors arm and spin correctly; all sensor readings nominal in configurator

problems.name: Water Damage
problems.description: The drone has been exposed to rain, water landing or submersion, causing corrosion or short-circuit damage to electronics.
visual_symptoms: Corrosion or white mineral deposits visible on PCB or connectors
Random ESC or motor errors after flying in rain
Short circuit symptoms: battery drains immediately when connected
Stiff or corroded motor bearings

probable_causes.cause: Liquid ingress shorting PCB traces on flight controller or ESC
probable_causes.test: Inspect PCB under magnification for corrosion, bridging or burnt spots

probable_causes.cause: Water in motor bearings causing rust and increased resistance
probable_causes.test: Spin each motor by hand to check for roughness; compare resistance with multimeter across windings

probable_causes.cause: Oxidation of connector contacts causing intermittent connections
probable_causes.test: Inspect all XT60 and JST connectors for green or white corrosion on contacts

steps.instruction: Immediately remove battery and do not reconnect power; disassemble drone to access all PCBs
steps.verification: Battery disconnected; all accessible boards removed from frame

steps.instruction: Clean all PCBs thoroughly with 99% isopropyl alcohol and a soft brush to remove water and mineral deposits
steps.verification: No visible corrosion or mineral deposits remaining; boards visually clean

steps.instruction: Place components in sealed bag with silica gel desiccant for 24 hours, then reassemble and power test
steps.verification: No short circuit detected; all components initialise correctly on power-on
